assertions.assertion: Code fetches the discovery document to resolve the introspection endpoint rather than hardcoding it
assertions.rationale: Code calls GetDiscoveryDocumentAsync and uses disco.IntrospectionEndpoint.

assertions.assertion: Code calls IntrospectTokenAsync (or POSTs to the introspection endpoint) with the API's ClientId and ClientSecret for authentication
assertions.rationale: Code calls IntrospectTokenAsync with ClientId = "catalog-api" and ClientSecret = "api-secret".

assertions.assertion: Code checks the IsActive property (or 'active' field) of the introspection response to determine if the token is valid
assertions.rationale: Code checks introspectionResponse.IsActive and returns 401 if not active.

assertions.assertion: Response explains that reference tokens are opaque identifiers (not self-contained JWTs) and require server-side validation via introspection
assertions.rationale: Opening section explains reference tokens are opaque identifiers stored server-side, requiring introspection.

assertions.assertion: Response mentions that reference tokens enable immediate revocation unlike JWTs which are valid until expiry
assertions.rationale: Dedicated subsection explains immediate revocation advantage and comparison table.
